title: Carlotta Angiolillo
text:
Hi! I’m a recent graduate of Northwestern’s Medill School of Journalism and a general assignment reporter at the Columbia Missourian. I’m looking for my next opportunity in journalism once my time with the Missourian ends in August 2026.
I wrote my first article when I was in fifth grade for my elementary school’s student newspaper, and I’ve been hooked on journalism ever since. There’s no doubt in my mind that it’s the best job in the world. The privilege to learn about and share people’s stories is one I treasure, and the responsibility to report the truth and hold power to account is one I take seriously.
I have experience in many different types of journalism, including audio storytelling, data reporting, investigative work, community engagement and legal reporting. I’m always looking for ways to learn new skills and grow as a journalist.
Outside of journalism, I’m usually taking a long walk, spending too much money at a coffee shop, learning new chords on the guitar or catching up on the latest episode of Abbott Elementary.
You can reach me at lottangiol@gmail.com or 832-728-7576.

title: Resume
text:
EDUCATION
Northwestern University | B.S. in Journalism | Minor in Data Science | September 2023 - June 2026
INTERNSHIPS AND FREELANCE WORK
The Columbia Missourian, Incoming Reporting Intern (Starting June 2026 through August 2026)

GA reporter via Scripps Howard Fund internship program; responsible for pitching, reporting and writing daily stories for Columbia, MO community

Chicago Public Media / WBEZ Chicago, Freelancer (September 2025 - December 2025)

Co-produced audio segment for April 12 Prisoncast! show with incarcerated students and WBEZ reporters

Houston Chronicle, Opinion Team Intern (June 2025 - August 2025)

Edited and fact checked contributed op-eds; solicited op-eds from diverse voices in the Houston area
Pitched, reported and wrote columns; published stories on WCM; promoted stories on X and Reddit

Covering Katy News, Freelance Reporter (June 2024 - September 2024)

Pitched and wrote 10+ hyperlocal stories; established source relationships with community members

NORTHWESTERN OPPORTUNITIES
The Daily Northwestern, Reporter (October 2023 - Present)

Cover campus and Evanston stories, photograph local events and sports games, analyze campus poll data

Medill News Service, Congressional Reporter - Health & Science (January 2026 - March 2026)

Covered health and science policy developments in D.C.; produced accurate reporting on tight deadlines

The Daily Northwestern, Features Editor (August 2025 - December 2025)

Edited, published one to three longform feature stories weekly, uploaded with FLEX Pro WordPress (SNO)
Managed publishing schedule, budget of story pitches and weekly check-ins with team of 25+ reporters

Data Journalism Collective, Co-President (June 2025 - May 2026)

Organized club meetings, workshops and speakers to increase student engagement with data journalism

The Daily Northwestern, Audience Engagement Editor (March 2025 - June 2025)

Collaborated with desk editors, social media team to increase engagement across digital platforms
Hosted community listening session to improve news coverage, communicated feedback to newsroom

The Daily Northwestern, Digital Managing Editor (August 2024 - December 2024)

Edited all content from seven digital-focused desks, including data visualization, audio, photo, newsletter

The Daily Northwestern, Audio Editor (March 2024 - June 2024)

Published one to three podcasts weekly, uploaded using SoundCloud, Spotify, Cyberduck, WordPress

SKILLS AND AWARDS

Proficient in AP Style, data analysis and visualization (R, Google Sheets, Flourish), Adobe Audition, Adobe Lightroom, photography, field recording (audio)
5th place in the ACP Multimedia Story of the Year (Podcast) contest (2024) for “A look into The Daily’s coverage of the Deering Meadow encampment”

title: Photojournalism
text:
During my time as a reporter and editor for The Daily Northwestern, I had the opportunity to do some sports photojournalism for various Northwestern sports, which was a fun challenge to take on. I also took pictures for stories I wrote for the paper. Check out some of my favorites below.









Paczki eating contest in Evanston, Illinois







Northwestern men’s basketball game versus Iowa
